title: Haiti decriminalizes same-sex sexual activity
description: Haiti removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex relations in 1986, ending their status as an offense under national law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ontario adds sexual orientation to its anti-discrimination law
description: The Canadian province of Ontario passed legislation in 1986 prohibiting discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Supreme Court upholds Georgia's sodomy law in Bowers v. Hardwick
description: On 30 June 1986 the US Supreme Court decided Bowers v. Hardwick, upholding Georgia's statute against oral and anal sex. The ruling held that the constitutional right to privacy did not cover same-sex relations, while leaving open whether the law applied to heterosexual couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First joint adoption granted to an openly lesbian couple in the US
description: In 1986 Becky Smith and Annie Afleck became the first openly lesbian couple in the United States to receive legal joint adoption of a child.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: ILGA Asia Conference held in Tokyo
description: The International Lesbian and Gay Association held its Asia Conference in Tokyo from 1 to 3 May 1986, bringing regional activists together.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Dutch Remonstrants become first Christian denomination to bless same-sex unions
description: In 1986 the Remonstrant Brotherhood in the Netherlands began performing same-sex unions and marriages, making it the first Christian denomination in the world to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hill Street Blues introduces first recurring lesbian character on US network TV
description: In 1986 the series Hill Street Blues featured police officer Kate McBride, played by Lindsay Crouse, the first recurring lesbian character on a major US television network.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: ACT UP founded in New York to fight government inaction on AIDS
description: The AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power (ACT UP) formed in the United States in 1987 as a direct-action group protesting the government's slow response to the AIDS crisis.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New York Area Bisexual Network founded
description: A group of New York bisexual activists including Brenda Howard established the New York Area Bisexual Network (NYABN) in 1987 to connect the city's bisexual community.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Boulder voters approve first US referendum banning anti-gay discrimination
description: Citizens of Boulder, Colorado passed a referendum in 1987 prohibiting discrimination based on sexual orientation, the first such ballot measure approved by voters in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Manitoba and Yukon adopt sexual-orientation discrimination protections
description: In 1987 the Canadian province of Manitoba and the territory of Yukon added protections against discrimination based on sexual orientation to their laws.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: ACT UP stages its first major demonstration
description: ACT UP held its first large protest in 1987, demanding faster government action on AIDS; seventeen demonstrators were arrested.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Barney Frank becomes first US congressman to come out voluntarily
description: In 1987 Massachusetts congressman Barney Frank publicly disclosed that he is gay, the first member of the US Congress to do so of his own accord.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Homomonument unveiled in Amsterdam
description: The Homomonument, a memorial honoring homosexuals persecuted through history, opened in Amsterdam in 1987.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: David Norris becomes first openly gay person elected to office in Ireland
description: In 1987 David Norris won election to public office in the Republic of Ireland, the country's first openly gay elected official.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First nationwide bisexual contingent marches on Washington
description: Seventy-five bisexual activists marched in the 1987 March on Washington for Gay and Lesbian Rights, the first nationwide bisexual gathering. Lani Ka'ahumanu's essay on the emerging bisexual movement appeared in the march's Civil Disobedience Handbook, the first such article carried by a national lesbian and gay publication.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sweden passes pioneering legal protections for same-sex couples
description: In 1988 Sweden became the first country to adopt laws protecting homosexuals in matters of social services, taxation, and inheritance.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Canada lowers the age of consent for sodomy to 18
description: Canadian law was amended in 1988 to reduce the age of consent for sodomy to 18, easing but not fully equalizing the rules applying to same-sex acts.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Belize listed as decriminalizing sex between men
description: The source timeline records Belize as removing criminal penalties for sexual acts between men in 1988. Other accounts date Belize's decriminalization to a 2016 court ruling, so the 1988 claim requires verification.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Israel repeals its ban on sex between men
description: In 1988 Israel formally decriminalized sexual acts between men by repealing a provision inherited from the 1936 British mandate code, a law the state had never actually enforced.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Britain enacts Section 28 against 'promotion of homosexuality'
description: In 1988 the anti-gay Section 28 became law in England and Wales, barring local authorities from 'promoting homosexuality'; Scotland enacted nearly identical legislation. The Scottish provision lasted until 2000 and the English-Welsh one until 2003.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Canadian MP Svend Robinson comes out
description: In 1988 Svend Robinson publicly came out, becoming Canada's first openly gay Member of Parliament.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: European Court of Human Rights strikes down Irish sodomy law in Norris v. Ireland
description: After losing before the Irish High Court in 1980 and the Irish Supreme Court in 1983, David Norris brought his case to the European Court of Human Rights, which ruled in 1988 that Ireland's criminalization of sex between men violated the right to privacy. Irish law nonetheless kept homosexuality illegal until 1993.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stacy Offner becomes first openly lesbian rabbi at a mainstream congregation
description: In 1988 the Reform congregation Shir Tikvah of Minneapolis hired Stacy Offner, the first openly lesbian rabbi employed by a mainstream Jewish congregation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Robert Dover becomes the first openly gay Olympic athlete
description: American equestrian Robert Dover competed as the first openly gay athlete at the Olympic Games in 1988.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Western Australia decriminalizes homosexuality
description: The Australian state of Western Australia removed criminal penalties for homosexuality in 1989, though it set the age of consent at 21.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Liechtenstein decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Liechtenstein removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex relations in 1989.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Denmark creates the world's first registered partnerships for same-sex couples
description: In 1989 Denmark became the first country to pass and implement a registered-partnership law for same-sex couples, granting most rights of marriage while excluding adoption (until June 2010) and church weddings.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New York court recognizes a gay couple as family in Braschi v. Stahl
description: In the 1989 case Braschi v. Stahl Associates Co., New York's highest court ruled that a gay couple who had shared a home for a decade counted as a family under the city's rent-control rules, holding that family protection should rest on the reality of family life rather than formal legal ties.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Czechoslovakia equalizes the age of consent
description: In 1990 Czechoslovakia set the same age of consent for same-sex and opposite-sex relations, a rule carried forward by its successor states, the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jersey decriminalizes homosexuality
description: The UK Crown Dependency of Jersey removed criminal penalties for homosexuality in 1990.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Queensland decriminalizes homosexuality
description: The Australian state of Queensland removed criminal penalties for homosexuality in 1990.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Queer Nation founded in the United States
description: Activists in the United States launched Queer Nation in 1990, a direct-action group confronting homophobia and anti-gay violence.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: OutRage! founded in the United Kingdom
description: The British direct-action group OutRage! formed in 1990 to campaign against discrimination and for LGBTQ rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Humsafar Trust founded in India
description: The Humsafar Trust, one of India's earliest organizations serving LGBTQ people, was established in 1990.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: World Health Organization removes homosexuality from its disease classification
description: On 17 May 1990 the World Health Organization stopped classifying homosexuality as an illness, dropping it from the International Classification of Diseases.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Justin Fashanu becomes the first professional footballer to come out
description: In 1990 British footballer Justin Fashanu came out in the press, the first professional player in the sport to do so publicly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Dale McCormick becomes first open lesbian elected to a US state Senate
description: In 1990 Dale McCormick won a seat in the Maine Senate, the first open lesbian elected to any US state Senate.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Reform Judaism declares lesbian and gay Jews full and equal members
description: In 1990 the Union for Reform Judaism adopted a national policy recognizing lesbian and gay Jews as full and equal members of the religious community. Its rabbinical body, the Central Conference of American Rabbis, endorsed a committee report concluding that all rabbis should be able to pursue their vocation regardless of sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: BiNet USA founded at the first US National Bisexual Conference
description: The oldest national bisexuality organization in the United States, BiNet USA, formed in 1990 under its original name, the North American Multicultural Bisexual Network. It held its first meeting at the first National Bisexual Conference in San Francisco, where over 450 people from 20 states and 5 countries gathered and the mayor proclaimed 23 June 1990 Bisexual Pride Day.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Eagle Creek Saloon opens as San Francisco's first black-owned gay bar
description: The Eagle Creek Saloon opened on the 1800 block of Market Street in San Francisco in 1990, the city's first black-owned gay bar; it closed in 1993.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The Bahamas decriminalizes homosexuality
description: The Bahamas removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex relations in 1991.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hong Kong decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Hong Kong removed criminal penalties for consensual sex between men in 1991.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ukraine decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Ukraine removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex relations in 1991, shortly after gaining independence.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Red ribbon debuts as the symbol of the fight against HIV/AIDS
description: In 1991 the red ribbon was used for the first time as a symbol of the campaign against HIV/AIDS, created by artists in New York.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nova Scotia bans discrimination based on sexual orientation
description: The Canadian province of Nova Scotia added sexual orientation to its anti-discrimination legislation in 1991.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Murder of Sonia Rescalvo Zafra in Barcelona
description: In 1991 neo-Nazi skinheads attacked and killed Sonia Rescalvo Zafra while she slept in the street in Barcelona, the first recognized transgender hate crime in Spain. Her murder is regarded as a turning point for the trans rights movement in Catalonia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sherry Harris becomes first openly lesbian African-American elected official
description: Sherry Harris won a seat on the Seattle City Council in 1991, making her the first openly lesbian African-American elected to public office in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First lesbian kiss airs on US television
description: In 1991 the series L.A. Law broadcast the first lesbian kiss on television, between the characters C.J. Lamb (Amanda Donohoe) and Abby (Michele Greene).

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chicago Gay and Lesbian Hall of Fame created
description: Chicago established its Gay and Lesbian Hall of Fame in June 1991, described as the first municipal institution of its kind in the United States and possibly the world.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: AB101 Veto Riot erupts in California
description: In 1991 protests broke out across California after Governor Pete Wilson vetoed Assembly Bill 101, which would have barred employers from discriminating against workers because of their sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Connie Norman hosts first daily gay-issues talk show on commercial radio in LA
description: In 1991 activist Connie Norman became the first gay rights advocate to host a daily talk show devoted to gay issues on a commercial station in the Los Angeles area.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Iceland equalizes the age of consent
description: Iceland set the same age of consent for same-sex and opposite-sex relations in 1992.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Luxembourg equalizes the age of consent
description: Luxembourg set the same age of consent for same-sex and opposite-sex relations in 1992.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Switzerland equalizes the age of consent
description: Switzerland set the same age of consent for same-sex and opposite-sex relations in 1992, following a national vote on the revised sexual criminal law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Estonia decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Estonia removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex relations in 1992, after regaining independence.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Latvia decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Latvia removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex relations in 1992, after regaining independence.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Isle of Man repeals its sodomy laws
description: The UK Crown Dependency of the Isle of Man repealed its sodomy provisions in 1992. The source notes that homosexuality remained restricted there until 1994, so the exact scope of the 1992 change requires verification.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Australia ends its ban on gay people in the military
description: Australia lifted its prohibition on gay and lesbian people serving in the armed forces in 1992.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Canada ends its ban on gay people in the military
description: Canada lifted its prohibition on gay and lesbian people serving in the armed forces in 1992.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nicaragua recriminalizes homosexuality
description: In 1992 Nicaragua reinstated criminal penalties for same-sex relations, a ban that remained in force until March 2008.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Brunswick and British Columbia ban sexual-orientation discrimination
description: In 1992 the Canadian provinces of New Brunswick and British Columbia added sexual orientation to their anti-discrimination legislation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
